publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-07 00:08:50 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Consists mainly of correspondence among American abolitionists, including many letters to Samuel Joseph May. Also included correspondence among members of May's family and the family of James Miller McKim; and some correspondence with Daniel Willard Fiske and George William Harris concerning the building of the May Anti-Slavery Collection at Cornell University.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Samuel Joseph May (1797-1871) was an American Unitarian minister and civil libertarian, one of the founders of the New England Anti-Slavery Society in 1832 and of the American Anti-Slavery Society in 1833; he also worked for women's rights.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Consists mainly of correspondence among American abolitionists, including many letters to Samuel Joseph May. Also included are May's diaries from 1859-1861 and 1865-1870; correspondence among members of May's family and the family of James Miller McKim; and some correspondence with Daniel Willard Fiske and George William Harris concerning the building of the May Anti-Slavery Collection at Cornell University. Correspondents include Lyman Abbott, John Brown, Henry Grafton Chapman, Maria Weston Chapman, David Lee Child, Lydia Maria Child, Daniel Willard Fiske, William Lloyd Garrison, D.C. Haynes, George William Harris, H.B. Holmes, Oliver Otis Howard, Oliver Johnson, Joseph May, Lucretia F. May, Samuel May Jr., Samuel Joseph May, James Miller McKim, William Forster Mitchell, Lucretia Coffin Mott, Wendell Phillips, Reuben Tomlinson, George Cabot Ward, and Theodore G. Wright.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Diaries and other bound manuscripts are cataloged separately and can be found by searching the catalog for collection number 4601.

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Large book and pamphlet collection concerning slavery and the anti-slavery movement also held in the repository, including slave narratives; abolitionist tracts, sermons, and letters; poetry and hymns, and documents of state governments and organizations concerning slavery.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: This collection came in as a series of donations and has therefore been organized by donation into these series: Maria and Henry Chapman papers, Lydia Maria Child papers, letters and documents from other abolitionists, and Freedmen's Aid Organization.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Researchers may view one folder at a time.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: May Anti-Slavery Collection, #4601. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Maria and Henry Chapman papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Journal of a voyage to Haiti

did.unittitle: Letter to Cornell about May Anti-Slavery collection, signed by Garrison, May, Smith, and Phillips
Series II. Lydia Maria Child papers
"Anne Whitney's Model of Charles Sumner; now in New York, and worthy of a place in its Central Park"
Copies of poems addressed to Lydia Maria Child
Duplicity - A tale from real life
How a very small Mouse helped to gnaw open a net that held a great Lion
Letter from New York…Boston Daily Courier, December 14, 1846
Poem in German by Graf von Loeben
"Satan in search of a second wife By his first Mother-in-law"
Short pieces to be put in to fill up pages if needed
"To the Spirit of my Beloved Old Mate"
Translation of a song by Heinrich Heine
Two poems in memory of Ellis Gray Loring
Copy of the Account of Wendell Phillips, Executor of the Will of Lydia Maria Child
Fifth and final account of estate
Third and final account of estate

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material was part of the donation given by James Miller McKim and later added to the May Collection

did.unittitle: Plan of Commission for the relief or National Freedmen, signed by Ward, G.C.
Drafts of Constitution of the Western Department of the American Freedmen's Aid Commission
Two records of the Bureau of Refugees, Freedmen, and Abandoned Lands
Copy of Minutes of the Executive Committee of the American Freedmen's Aid Commission
Minutes of "North Western Freedman's Aid", Chicago meeting
Two Certificates for delegates to represent The Northwestern Freedmen's Aud Commission at the two conventions held in D.C.
Letter by Abbott, Lyman to Rev. Walden, with list of the General Agents of the Freedmen Commission
Board of Directors of the North Western Freedmen's Aid Commission, with list of names
Two lists of State Superintendents of Education
"Monthly Report of a Colored School…", known as the Mattapan School in Eastville, Virginia
Supplemental Report of E and M. A. Darrow account
Statement of the condition of the 75 schools in South Carolina byt the State Superintendent of Education
Consolidated State Superintendent Monthly School Report to Bureau of Refugees, Freedmen, and Abandoned Lands
New York Branch Freedmen's Union Commission Teacher's school report
Four identical blank forms of schedule of schools and rental accounts
Three reports of Collecting Agents of the North Western Freedmen's Aid Commission
Notification about the transfer to the Association of School Buildings
Letters from General Supt. Schools, regarding accounts, reports, forms, etc.
Fourteen pages of a preliminary financial statement covering schools in charge of the commission
Printed circular, "How can American Slavery be Abolished?"
Printed circular giving an account of the work so far performed, with a program for the future
Announcement of the opening of an office of the American Freedmen's Aid Commission in D.C., signed Shipher, Jacob R.
Printed circular, meeting to be held in D.C.
(facsimile) National Freedmen's Aid Union, "Lancashire Dirstress Surplus Fund"
Bureau of Refugees, Freedmen, and Abandoned Lands transportation orders for teachers form
Bureau of Refugees, Freedmen, and Abandoned Lands transportation order for Mrs. S.O Snelling
Three transportation orders in the name of Mrs. S. O. Snelling
Six transportation orders in the name of Phineas Colver
Circular Letter No. 1, Maj. Gen. O.O. Howard announces appointment
Article of Agreement: Freedmen's Orphans Home Indenture of Narcissa McNeil
Western Sanitary Commission letter from Rev. J. Forman to Walden, J.M.
T. Harrison enclosing applications for teachers as Arkadephia and letters
"Relation between the Memphis Colored Orphan Asylum and the Northwest Freedmen Commission" letters by Canfield, S.A.M. to Walden, Rev. J.M.
Two letters by Abbot, Lyman to Eddy, T.M. and Walden, J.M.
Copy of contract "imposed upon freedmen who could not write…"
Old MSS numbers 49-60: Letters and fliers regarding the building of the May Anti-Slavery Collection
Document from "The undersigned citizens of Philadelphia…" protesting admission of Texas to the Union

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lawrence, Fannie Virginia Casseopia; Child, Lydia Maria Francis; Douglass, Frederick; Garrison, William Lloyd; Giddings, Joshua Reed; Phillips, Wendell; Seward, William H; Smith, Gerrit

did.unittitle: Three signed petitions from Massachusetts to end slave hunting and slave trade
Certificate of search for slaves on board a vessel
Series IV. Correspondence and Documents from Other Abolitionists

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Correspondence is arranged alphabetically by author. Documents are arranged by author or associated name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed letter asking Robinson to collect donations pledged to the local (Cincinnati) anti-slavery society. Bailey notes that anti-slavery sentiment is growing in the community, and adds that the coming session of Congress may bring decisive changes. On verso of second leaf is a list of those who have pledged donations but have not yet paid. Also, an autograph clipped from a letter from Bailey to an unidentified friend, dated March 14, 1838.

George; Miss Wisham
